+    func testApplyingHotkeyBindingUsesUpdateCallbackAndClearsErrors() throws {
+        let settings = makeSettings()
+        let updates = SettingsShellHotkeyUpdateRecorder()
+        let chord = try HotkeyChord(keys: [.leftCommand, .leftControl])
+        let controller = makeController(
+            settingsStore: settings,
+            updateHotkeyBinding: { updatedChord, action in
+                updates.calls.append(.init(action: action, chord: updatedChord))
+                try settings.updateHotkeyBinding(updatedChord, for: action)
+            }
+        )
+
+        controller.applyHotkeyBinding(chord, for: .pushToTalk)
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(updates.calls, [.init(action: .pushToTalk, chord: chord)])
+        XCTAssertEqual(controller.pushToTalkChord, chord)
+        XCTAssertNil(controller.hotkeyBindingErrorMessage)
+    }
+
+    func testApplyingDuplicateHotkeyBindingLeavesThePreviousChordAndShowsError() throws {
+        let controller = makeController(
+            updateHotkeyBinding: { _, _ in
+                throw HotkeyBindingStoreError.duplicateBinding(action: .toggleToTalk)
+            }
+        )
+        let originalChord = controller.pushToTalkChord
+
+        controller.applyHotkeyBinding(controller.toggleToTalkChord, for: .pushToTalk)
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(controller.pushToTalkChord, originalChord)
+        XCTAssertEqual(
+            controller.hotkeyBindingErrorMessage,
+            "That shortcut is already assigned to Toggle Recording."
+        )
+    }
+
+    func testShortcutCaptureLifecycleDelegatesToParentCallbacksAndClearsBindingErrors() throws {
+        let lifecycle = SettingsShellShortcutCaptureRecorder()
+        let controller = makeController(
+            updateHotkeyBinding: { _, _ in
+                throw HotkeyBindingStoreError.duplicateBinding(action: .toggleToTalk)
+            },
+            beginShortcutCapture: {
+                lifecycle.events.append(.begin)
+            },
+            endShortcutCapture: {
+                lifecycle.events.append(.end)
+            }
+        )
+
+        controller.applyHotkeyBinding(controller.toggleToTalkChord, for: .pushToTalk)
+        XCTAssertNotNil(controller.hotkeyBindingErrorMessage)
+
+        controller.beginShortcutCapture()
+        controller.endShortcutCapture()
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(lifecycle.events, [.begin, .end])
+        XCTAssertNil(controller.hotkeyBindingErrorMessage)
+    }
+
     func testLaunchAtLoginUsesReturnedFinalState() {
         let controller = makeController(updateLaunchAtLogin: { _ in false })

@@ -0,0 +1,95 @@
+import XCTest
+@testable import GhostPepper
+
+@MainActor
+final class ShortcutRecorderControllerTests: XCTestCase {
+    func testBeginRecordingStartsCaptureAndShowsPromptState() throws {
+        let events = ShortcutRecorderLifecycleRecorder()
+        let controller = try makeController(events: events)
+
+        controller.beginRecording()
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(events.events, [.begin])
+        XCTAssertTrue(controller.isRecording)
+        XCTAssertEqual(controller.buttonTitle, "Press Shortcut")
+        XCTAssertTrue(controller.showsInstructions)
+    }
+
+    func testRecordingPreviewUpdatesAndCommitSavesChord() throws {
+        let events = ShortcutRecorderLifecycleRecorder()
+        var changedChords: [HotkeyChord] = []
+        let controller = try makeController(
+            events: events,
+            onChange: { changedChords.append($0) }
+        )
+
+        controller.beginRecording()
+        controller.handleModifierChange(.rightCommand)
+        controller.handleModifierChange(.rightOption)
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(controller.displayedKeyLabels, ["⌘ʳ", "⌥ʳ"])
+
+        controller.handleModifierChange(.rightCommand)
+        controller.handleModifierChange(.rightOption)
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(events.events, [.begin, .end])
+        XCTAssertFalse(controller.isRecording)
+        XCTAssertEqual(changedChords, [try HotkeyChord(keys: [.rightCommand, .rightOption])])
+        XCTAssertEqual(controller.currentChord, try HotkeyChord(keys: [.rightCommand, .rightOption]))
+    }
+
+    func testEscapeBeforeAnyCapturedKeysCancelsWithoutChangingTheChord() throws {
+        let events = ShortcutRecorderLifecycleRecorder()
+        var didChange = false
+        let controller = try makeController(
+            events: events,
+            onChange: { _ in didChange = true }
+        )
+        let originalChord = controller.currentChord
+
+        controller.beginRecording()
+        controller.handleKeyDown(.escape)
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(events.events, [.begin, .end])
+        XCTAssertFalse(controller.isRecording)
+        XCTAssertFalse(didChange)
+        XCTAssertEqual(controller.currentChord, originalChord)
+    }
+
+    func testDisappearCancelsAnActiveRecording() throws {
+        let events = ShortcutRecorderLifecycleRecorder()
+        let controller = try makeController(events: events)
+
+        controller.beginRecording()
+        controller.disappear()
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(events.events, [.begin, .end])
+        XCTAssertFalse(controller.isRecording)
+    }
+
+    private func makeController(
+        events: ShortcutRecorderLifecycleRecorder,
+        onChange: @escaping @MainActor (HotkeyChord) -> Void = { _ in }
+    ) throws -> ShortcutRecorderController {
+        ShortcutRecorderController(
+            currentChord: try HotkeyChord(keys: [.leftCommand, .leftControl]),
+            beginCapture: {
+                events.events.append(.begin)
+            },
+            endCapture: {
+                events.events.append(.end)
+            },
+            onChange: onChange
+        )
+    }
+}
+
+@MainActor
+private final class ShortcutRecorderLifecycleRecorder {
+    enum Event: Equatable {
+        case begin
+        case end
+    }
+
+    var events: [Event] = []
+}

@@ -80,3 +80,13 @@
 - Wired the settings surface into the real shell dependencies so model selection, cleanup download/error visibility, launch-at-login toggling, microphone device listing, screen-recording recovery guidance, and in-window test dictation all run against the existing app services.
 - Added reusable cleanup prompt and cleanup transcript utility windows with persistent hide-and-reuse behavior, first-responder commit before hide, and focused shell/controller coverage for the new settings flow.
 **Summary:** Delivered the real settings shell and utility-window layer on top of Ghost Pepper's existing runtime services. Ghost Pepper now opens a persistent settings window from the menu bar, defaults to the Recording section, exposes the documented configuration cards across Recording, Cleanup, Corrections, Models, and General, refreshes device, permission, and history state whenever the window appears, surfaces runtime model inventory and cleanup download/error state, and ships reusable prompt/transcript windows that preserve edits when hidden. During wrap-up, the audit moved shortcut-recorder stories STORY-0928, STORY-0951, and STORY-0955 to ITER-0008 because they depend on the dedicated shortcut-capture iteration, and moved STORY-0952 to ITER-0012 because learned corrections require the later adaptive-learning subsystem.
+
+## ITER-0008
+
+**Completed:** 2026-04-10
+**Stories delivered:** STORY-0556, STORY-0557, STORY-0558, STORY-0559, STORY-0560, STORY-0561, STORY-0562, STORY-0563, STORY-0564, STORY-0565, STORY-0566, STORY-0567, STORY-0568, STORY-0569, STORY-0570, STORY-0571, STORY-0572, STORY-0573, STORY-0574, STORY-0575, STORY-0576, STORY-0577, STORY-0578, STORY-0579, STORY-0580, STORY-0581, STORY-0582, STORY-0583, STORY-0584, STORY-0585, STORY-0586, STORY-0587, STORY-0588, STORY-0589, STORY-0590, STORY-0591, STORY-0592, STORY-0593, STORY-0594, STORY-0928, STORY-0951, STORY-0955
+**Tasks executed:**
+- Finished the hotkey-binding domain with the documented physical-key catalog, chord display formatting, duplicate-binding rejection, JSON-backed binding persistence, and the shortcut capture state machine.
+- Added the reusable shortcut recorder widget with live chord preview, local key-event interception, reusable key-cap styling, and settings-shell integration for the Hold to Record and Toggle Recording controls.
+- Hardened shortcut-capture lifecycle handling by suspending the global hotkey monitor during recording, rebuilding bindings on successful save, and suppressing resumed hotkey matching until held keys are released.
+**Summary:** Delivered the full shortcut customization slice on top of the existing settings shell. Ghost Pepper now exposes two recorder widgets in Settings > Recording, previews captured keys as you press them, saves non-conflicting bindings back into `UserDefaults`, shows duplicate-binding errors inline, and keeps global hotkeys from interfering with capture or retriggering while held keys unwind after recording.

 ### ITER-0009 — Cleanup backend and pipeline
